• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • Download Rmd

Mice

Make metadata

Audrey

2023-09-14

This file is used to generate :

  • sample_info : dataframe containing sample names, sample path, custom colors…
  • color_markers : named list to give custom colors to cell types
  • cell_markers : named list containing markers for each cell type
library(dplyr)
library(patchwork)
library(ggplot2)

.libPaths()
## [1] "/usr/local/lib/R/library"

(Time to run : 0.82 s)

Preparation

In this section, we set the global settings of the analysis. We will store data there :

save_name = "mice"
out_dir = "."

Sample info

We create a dataframe containing everything except colors.

fl/fl

sample_info_flfl = data.frame(
  project_name = c("2020_09", "2020_17", "2019_H", "2020_24", "2020_13",
                   "2020_14", "2019_A", "2020_02", "2019_C", "2020_18",
                   "2019_J", "2019_I", "2019_D", "2020_19", "2019_G", "2021_33"),
  genotype = c(rep("flfl", 16)),
  tumor_type = c(rep("pNF", 2), rep("dyNF", 10), rep("MPNST", 4)),
  gender = "M",
  color = NA,
  origin = "here",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_type = paste0(genotype, "_", tumor_type)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(unique_id = data.table::rowid(sample_type)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_identifier = paste0(sample_type, "_", unique_id)) %>%
  dplyr::select(project_name,  genotype, tumor_type, gender,
                origin, sample_identifier, sample_type, color)

sample_info_flfl
##    project_name genotype tumor_type gender origin sample_identifier sample_type
## 1       2020_09     flfl        pNF      M   here        flfl_pNF_1    flfl_pNF
## 2       2020_17     flfl        pNF      M   here        flfl_pNF_2    flfl_pNF
## 3        2019_H     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_1   flfl_dyNF
## 4       2020_24     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_2   flfl_dyNF
## 5       2020_13     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_3   flfl_dyNF
## 6       2020_14     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_4   flfl_dyNF
## 7        2019_A     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_5   flfl_dyNF
## 8       2020_02     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_6   flfl_dyNF
## 9        2019_C     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_7   flfl_dyNF
## 10      2020_18     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_8   flfl_dyNF
## 11       2019_J     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_9   flfl_dyNF
## 12       2019_I     flfl       dyNF      M   here      flfl_dyNF_10   flfl_dyNF
## 13       2019_D     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_1  flfl_MPNST
## 14      2020_19     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_2  flfl_MPNST
## 15       2019_G     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_3  flfl_MPNST
## 16      2021_33     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_4  flfl_MPNST
##    color
## 1     NA
## 2     NA
## 3     NA
## 4     NA
## 5     NA
## 6     NA
## 7     NA
## 8     NA
## 9     NA
## 10    NA
## 11    NA
## 12    NA
## 13    NA
## 14    NA
## 15    NA
## 16    NA

fl/-

sample_info_flmn = data.frame(
  project_name = c("2022_25", "2022_26", "2022_30"),
  genotype = c(rep("flmn", 3)),
  tumor_type = c(rep("MPNST", 3)),
  gender = "M",
  color = NA,
  origin = "here",
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_type = paste0(genotype, "_", tumor_type)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(unique_id = data.table::rowid(sample_type)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_identifier = paste0(sample_type, "_", unique_id)) %>%
  dplyr::select(project_name,  genotype, tumor_type, gender,
                origin, sample_identifier, sample_type, color)

sample_info_flmn
##   project_name genotype tumor_type gender origin sample_identifier sample_type
## 1      2022_25     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_1  flmn_MPNST
## 2      2022_26     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_2  flmn_MPNST
## 3      2022_30     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_3  flmn_MPNST
##   color
## 1    NA
## 2    NA
## 3    NA

Colors

We merge all four dataframes :

sample_info = rbind(sample_info_flfl,
                    sample_info_flmn) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(tumor_type = factor(tumor_type, levels = c("pNF", "dyNF", "MPNST"))) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(genotype, tumor_type, gender) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(project_name = factor(project_name, levels = project_name)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(sample_identifier = factor(sample_identifier, levels = sample_identifier))
sample_info
##    project_name genotype tumor_type gender origin sample_identifier sample_type
## 1       2020_09     flfl        pNF      M   here        flfl_pNF_1    flfl_pNF
## 2       2020_17     flfl        pNF      M   here        flfl_pNF_2    flfl_pNF
## 3        2019_H     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_1   flfl_dyNF
## 4       2020_24     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_2   flfl_dyNF
## 5       2020_13     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_3   flfl_dyNF
## 6       2020_14     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_4   flfl_dyNF
## 7        2019_A     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_5   flfl_dyNF
## 8       2020_02     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_6   flfl_dyNF
## 9        2019_C     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_7   flfl_dyNF
## 10      2020_18     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_8   flfl_dyNF
## 11       2019_J     flfl       dyNF      M   here       flfl_dyNF_9   flfl_dyNF
## 12       2019_I     flfl       dyNF      M   here      flfl_dyNF_10   flfl_dyNF
## 13       2019_D     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_1  flfl_MPNST
## 14      2020_19     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_2  flfl_MPNST
## 15       2019_G     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_3  flfl_MPNST
## 16      2021_33     flfl      MPNST      M   here      flfl_MPNST_4  flfl_MPNST
## 17      2022_25     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_1  flmn_MPNST
## 18      2022_26     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_2  flmn_MPNST
## 19      2022_30     flmn      MPNST      M   here      flmn_MPNST_3  flmn_MPNST
##    color
## 1     NA
## 2     NA
## 3     NA
## 4     NA
## 5     NA
## 6     NA
## 7     NA
## 8     NA
## 9     NA
## 10    NA
## 11    NA
## 12    NA
## 13    NA
## 14    NA
## 15    NA
## 16    NA
## 17    NA
## 18    NA
## 19    NA

We add custom colors for each sample :

list_palette = list(
  # fl/fl
  flfl_pNF = colorRampPalette(c("deepskyblue", "dodgerblue")),
  flfl_dyNF = colorRampPalette(c("palegreen", "forestgreen")),
  flfl_MPNST = colorRampPalette(c("burlywood", "chocolate4")),
  # fl/-
  flmn_MPNST = colorRampPalette(c("orange", "orange4")))

for (sample_type in unique(sample_info$sample_type)) {
  matching_dataset = (sample_info$sample_type == sample_type)
  sample_info[matching_dataset, "color"] = list_palette[[sample_type]](sum(matching_dataset))
}

It is possible to make confusion between colors ?

graphics::pie(rep(1, nrow(sample_info)),
              col = sample_info$color,
              labels = sample_info$project_name)

We save it :

saveRDS(sample_info, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_sample_info.rds"))

Cell types

We define markers for each population of interest :

cell_markers = list(
  "tumor cells" = c("Ank3", "Plp1", "Nav2", "Postn", "Serpine2",
                    "Gpm6b", "Cald1", "Pdgfa", "Timp3", "Fbln2", 
                    "Lgi4", "Nr2f2", "Itgb8", "Col5a2", "Ptprz1", 
                    "Gjc3", "Tenm3", "Col1a2", "Zfp536", "Dlgap1", "Tead1", 
                    "Stard13", "Col27a1", "Cdh19", "Sox10", "Tanc2", "Erbb3",
                    "Col1a1", "Col3a1", "Pdlim4", "Klf9", 
                    "Hs2st1", "Gas7", "Utrn", "Specc1", "Frmd4a", "Tpm1", 
                    "Dusp6", "Gas1", "Cxcl12", "Pdgfrb", "Col6a1", "Htra1", 
                    "Slit2", "Pcolce", "Cpe", "Dkk2", 
                    "Prss23", "Gja1", "Nrep", "Nid1", 
                    "Itgbl1", "Pcdh19", "Ddr2", "Sdc2", 
                    "Mxra7", "Cnn3", "Ltbp1", "Foxg1",
                    "Rbfox2", "Tpm2", "Ryk", "Pxdn", 
                    "Kcna1", "Chl1", "Aatk", "Kcna2", 
                    "Cadm4", "Art3", "Cd59a", "Mal", "Cadm1", 
                    "Atp1a2", "Ptn", "Gatm", "Cnp", "Cryab", "Vwa1", 
                    "Emp2", "Prnp", "dtTomato", "tdTomato", "Pou3f1", "Mbp", 
                    "Scn7a", "Ckb", "Scd2", "Fabp5"),
  
  "macrophages" = c("C1qc", "Mafb", "Csf1r", "Fcgr3", "Trem2", "Cx3cr1", "Tmem176a", "Cybb", 
                    "Fcgr1", "Adgre1", "Trf", "Lst1", "Itgb5", "Ms4a6d", "Clec4a3", "Tlr2", 
                    "Ccl9", "Cebpa", "Apobec1", "C3ar1", "Pla2g7", "Klra2", "Clec4a1", "Sirpa", 
                    "Cd68", "Itgam", "Clec4a2", "Lair1", "AI607873", "Ifi207", "Ncf2", "C1qa", 
                    "Pid1", "Slc15a3", "Dab2", "Cxcl16", "Clec12a", "Lpcat2", "Hck", "Lrp1", 
                    "Ifngr2", "Ctsl", "Pirb", "Ccr1", "Lilrb4a", "Rassf4", "Ets2", "P2rx4", 
                    "Gngt2", "Syk", "Irf5"),
  
  "cDC" = c("Xcr1", "Itgae", "Clec9a", "Tlr3", "Sept3", "Septin3", "Cldn1", 
            "Btla", "Ffar4", "Ifi205", "Hepacam2", "Gcsam", "Mycl", "P2ry14", "Wdfy4", 
            "Anpep", "H2-Ob", "Mctp1", "Ece1", "Slamf7", "Shtn1", "Rab7b", 
            "Qpct", "Tbc1d8", "Batf3", "Naga", "Ppm1m", 
            "Dpp4", "Sdad1", "BC028528", "Fgd2", "Tbc1d9", "Pbx1", 
            "Rab32", "Slc25a20", "Fchsd2", "Klrb1b", "Gm6377", "Slamf8", "Erlin1", "Trim35", 
            "Slc8b1", "Bri3bp", "Havcr2", "Cdk14", "Acvrl1", "Arsb", 
            "Mnda", "Ifi211", "Pafah1b3", "Prkar2a", "Grasp", "Tamalin", "Arhgap5",
            "March1", "Marchf1", "Gng12", "Ppfia4"),
  
  "mDC" = c("Cd209a", "Mgl2", "Tnip3", "Mcemp1", "Cfp", "Olfm1", "Wfdc17", "Itgax", 
            "Napsa", "Alcam", "Spint1", "Eps8",
            "Cd300a", "Cysltr1", "Tep1", "Gpr35", "Ms4a4c", "Scimp"),
  
  "pDC" = c("Ly6d", "Cox6a2", "Ccr9", "Dntt", "Klk1", 
            "D13Ertd608e", "Spib", "Upb1", "Cd300c", "Sh3bgr", 
            "Paqr5", "Atp2a1", "Pir", "Siglech", "Smim5", 
            "Lefty1", "Klra17", "Atp1b1", "Lrrc16a", "Carmil1", 
            "Fcrla", "Pacsin1", "Mctp2", "Spns3", "Bcl11a", 
            "Cmah", "Il7r", "Tex2", "Gm5547", "Hpse", 
            "Blnk", "Cxxc5", "Dirc2", "Slc49a4", "Rell1", 
            "Sell", "Eepd1", "Runx2", "Ly6c2", "Ddr1", 
            "Nucb2", "Kmo", "Pltp", 
            "Sla2", "Plac8", "Csf2rb2", "Lifr", 
            "Abhd17b", "Hvcn1", "Prkca", "Clec10a", "Tmem229b", 
            "Tsc22d1", "Rpgrip1", "Sqle", "Lag3", 
            "Plekhm3", "Slc44a2", "Traf4", "Nceh1", "Cdkn2d", 
            "5430427O19Rik", "Tasl", "Net1", "Msmo1", "Tubgcp5", "Dclre1c", 
            "Atp2b4", "Khk", "Ptprs", "Idi1", 
            "Card11", "Scpep1", "5730508B09Rik", "Fam241a", "Jakmip1", "Tifa", 
            "Clec2i", "Man2a2", "Cd7", "Dctn6", "Fdps", 
            "Ctse", "Gpr137b", "Spint2", "Edem2", "Fgr", 
            "Gna15", "Pkig", "Cd38", 
            "Ostm1", "Creld2", "Srebf2", "Slc35c2"),
  
  "T cells" = c("Cd3g", "Nkg7", "Cd3d", "Cd3e", "Cd8a", "Cxcr6", "Ms4a4b",
                "Gimap4", "Gimap3", "Sh2d2a", "Cd28", "Cd2", "Lck", "Lat", "Bcl11b", 
                "Itk", "Gimap1", "Skap1", "Il2rb", "Ctsw", "Rhoh", 
                "Sept1", "Septin1", "Ctla2a"),
  
  "NK cells" = c("Klrb1c", "Ncr1", "Klra7", "Klrb1f", "Styk1", "Xcl1", "Klre1", "Klrc2", 
                 "Car2", "Samd3", "Spry2", "Satb1", "Klrk1", "Klrd1", "Txk", "Ctla2b", 
                 "Serpinb9", "Cst7", "Gem", "Eomes", "Cd244", "Cd244a", "Stat4", "Fasl", "Gimap5"),
  
  "neutrophils" = c("S100a9", "S100a8", "Cxcr2", "Csf3r", "Il1r2", "Trem1", "Clec4e", 
                    "Ptgs2", "Gsr", "Retnlg"),
  
  "mast cells" = c("Mrgprx2", "Tpsb2", "Tpsab1", "Mrgprb1", "Mcpt4", "Mrgprb2", "Fcer1a", 
                   "Cpa3", "Slc6a4", "Cma1", "Kit", "Rab27b", "Gata2", "Il1rl1", 
                   "Serpinb1a", "Lat2", "Vwa5a", "Ccl7", "Tmem64", "Basp1", 
                   "Ccl2", "Lilr4b", "ENSMUSG00000089672", "Gch1", "Fnip1"),
  
  "B cells" = c("Cd79a", "Ms4a1", "Bank1", "Fcmr", "Ccr7", "Cd79b",
                "Cd37", "H2-DMb2", "Ltb",
                "Scd1", "Gm26740", "Cytip", "Rel", "Pou2f2"),
  
  "endothelial cells" = c("Egfl7", "Cdh5", "Emcn", "Esam", "Plvap", "Aplnr", "Ramp2", 
                          "Tshz2", "Sox18", "Ecscr", "Adgrf5", "Ptprb", "Rasip1", "Tie1", 
                          "Kdr", "Cyyr1", "Mmrn2", "Grrp1", "Fam110d", "Flt1", "Arhgap29", "Lrg1",
                          "Vwf", "Kank3", "Nfib", "Eng", 
                          "Crip2", "Pecam1", "Abcg2", "Cd200", "Apbb2",
                          "Cavin3", "Mcam", "Insr", "Smad1", "Cd93", 
                          "Plekhg1", "Plxnd1", "Snrk"),
  
  "fibroblasts" = c("Mfap5", "Lum", "Serping1", "Cilp", "Cdh11", 
                    "Ccdc80", "Svep1", "Il33", "Cpxm1", 
                    "Fbn2", "Cpxm2", "Clec11a", "Fndc1", 
                    "Cd248", "Pdpn", "Slit3", "Fibin", "Colec12", "Pdgfrl", 
                    "Gxylt2", "Igsf10", "Snhg18", "Fkbp11", 
                    "Ctsk", "Adamts2", "Fzd2", "Mmp23", 
                    "Scarf2", "Kdelr3", "Fbn1", "Gpx3", 
                    "Mrc2", "P3h3", "Cdc42ep5", 
                    "Prrx1", "Rbp1", "Rhoj", 
                    "Col6a2", "Efemp2", "Col6a3", "Fzd1", 
                    "Lpar1", "Dpysl3", "Palld", 
                    "Bmp1", "Igfbp6", "Fstl1", "Fkbp10", "Maged2", "Crtap", 
                    "Rbms3", "Col5a1", "Plod2", "Oaf", "Grb10", "Copz2", 
                    "Plat", "Timp1", "Rab34", 
                    "Serf1", "Thbs2", "Npdc1", "Fam114a1", "Fscn1", "Meg3", 
                    "Ecm1", "Ddah2",
                    "Clec3b", "Cxcl14", "Cfh", "Gdf10", "Cldn1", "Fxyd6",
                    "Ifi205", "Pi16", "Lbp", "Bnc2", "Sned1", "Cttnbp2",
                    "Fbxl18", "Nr2f2", "Itga6", "Enpp2", "Gpc3", "Ccl11", "Vit", "Smoc2"),
  
  "mural cells" = c("Des", "Myh11", "Ano1", "Ndufa4l2", "Rgs5", "Notch3", "Gucy1a3", "Gucy1a1", "Pde3a", 
                    "Mylk", "Nrarp", "Apold1", "Zak", "Map3k20", "Gm13889", "Tinagl1", "Myl9", 
                    "Arhgef17", "Usp2", "Rasd1", "Myo1b", "Mustn1", "S1pr3", "Ptp4a3", 
                    "Slc25a25", "Ppp1r12b", "Rasl11a", "Rbpms", "Mfge8", "Adamts9", "Mgst3", 
                    "Rasgrp2", "Rrad", "Crispld2", "Epas1", "Mprip", "Hspb1", 
                    "Zfhx3", "Bicd2", "Syne2", "Cwc25", "Crtc3", "Nolc1", "Lpp", "Dlc1", 
                    "Mapre2", "Cygb", "Mafk", "Nudt4", "Tns1", "Irs2",
                    "Rbm4b", "Fbxo33", "Arhgef12"),
  
  "skeletal muscle cells" = c("Pgam2", "Apobec2", "Smpx", "Car3", "Tcap", "Cox8b", "Myoz1", "Eef1a2", 
                              "Pdlim3", "Klhl41", "Mb", "Fabp3", "Hspb6", "Myl1",
                              "Ckm", "Rpl3l", "Mylpf", "Ttn", "Eno3", "Tnnt3", 
                              "Jsrp1", "Tnnc2", "Tnni2", "Neb", "Acta1", "Cox7a1", 
                              "Tcea3", "Tuba4a", "Cystm1", "Adssl1", "Acyp2"))

lengths(cell_markers)
##           tumor cells           macrophages                   cDC 
##                    88                    51                    58 
##                   mDC                   pDC               T cells 
##                    18                    94                    24 
##              NK cells           neutrophils            mast cells 
##                    25                    10                    25 
##               B cells     endothelial cells           fibroblasts 
##                    14                    39                    87 
##           mural cells skeletal muscle cells 
##                    54                    31

Here are custom colors for each cell type :

color_markers = aquarius::color_markers
color_markers = color_markers[names(color_markers) %in% names(cell_markers)]
cell_markers = cell_markers[names(color_markers)] # re-order

data.frame(cell_type = names(color_markers),
           color = unlist(color_markers)) %>%
  ggplot2::ggplot(., aes(x = cell_type, y = 0, fill = cell_type)) +
  ggplot2::geom_point(pch = 21, size = 5) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = unlist(color_markers), breaks = names(color_markers)) +
  ggplot2::theme_classic() +
  ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "none",
                 axis.line = element_blank(),
                 axis.title = element_blank(),
                 axis.ticks = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.y = element_blank(),
                 axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 10))

These are some markers to assess cell type annotation :

dotplot_markers = list(
  "tumor cells" = c("dtTomato", "Plp1", "Sox10", "Sox9", "Pdgfra"),
  "macrophages" = c("Lyz2", "Adgre1"),
  "cDC" = c("Xcr1", "Itgae"),
  "mDC" = c("Cd209a", "Mgl2"),
  "pDC" = c("Ly6d", "Siglech"),
  "T cells" = c("Cd3e", "Cd8a", "Cd4"),
  "NK cells" = c("Klrb1c", "Ncr1"),
  "neutrophils" = c("S100a9", "Retnlg"),
  "mast cells" = c("Tpsb2", "Kit"),
  "B cells" = c("Cd79a", "Cd19"),
  "endothelial cells" = c("Egfl7", "Pecam1"),
  "fibroblasts" = c("Mfap5", "Lum"),
  "mural cells" = c("Notch3", "Rgs5"),
  "skeletal muscle cells" = c("Tnnt3", "Myl1"))

lengths(dotplot_markers)
##           tumor cells           macrophages                   cDC 
##                     5                     2                     2 
##                   mDC                   pDC               T cells 
##                     2                     2                     3 
##              NK cells           neutrophils            mast cells 
##                     2                     2                     2 
##               B cells     endothelial cells           fibroblasts 
##                     2                     2                     2 
##           mural cells skeletal muscle cells 
##                     2                     2

We save them :

saveRDS(color_markers, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_color_markers.rds"))
saveRDS(cell_markers, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_cell_markers.rds"))
saveRDS(dotplot_markers, file = paste0(out_dir, "/", save_name, "_dotplot_markers.rds"))

R Session

show 
## R version 3.6.3 (2020-02-29)
## Platform: x86_64-pc-linux-gnu (64-bit)
## Running under: Ubuntu 20.04.6 LTS
## 
## Matrix products: default
## BLAS:   /usr/local/lib/R/lib/libRblas.so
